topic: Someone learns their favorite author plagiarized their stories
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elias stared at the screen, his knuckles white where he gripped the mouse. The words swam before his eyes, a grotesque echo of his own carefully crafted prose. He reread the passage, the familiar cadence of his voice twisted into something alien, something cheap and borrowed. A slow, burning heat rose in his chest, tightening his throat until he could barely draw a breath. He slammed his laptop shut with a sharp crack that echoed in the quiet study. He stood up, pacing the worn rug, his movements jerky and agitated. Every shadow seemed to shift, every silence amplified into a deafening accusation. He needed air, needed distance, needed to scrub this sour taste from his mouth.

[story 2]

"This is impossible," Clara whispered, the sound brittle and thin in the cavernous library. She traced the lines of the copied text with a trembling finger, the paper feeling like sandpaper under her touch. A fierce, cold energy coiled beneath her skin, vibrating with suppressed fury. Her jaw locked so tightly she could feel the strain in her molars. She snatched the manuscript back, crumpling the pages with unnecessary violence. Her breath hitched, coming in short, sharp gasps. She spun around, her eyes blazing with a raw, untamed intensity, searching the empty room for the source of this betrayal.

[story 3]

I watched the footnotes, the subtle shifts in metaphor, and a sickening realization washed over me. It was *mine*. Every carefully chosen phrase, every intricate plot twist—it was all there, meticulously lifted, stitched together by some other hand. A tremor started in my hands, a rapid, uncontrollable shaking that made the desk rattle. I pushed back from the chair, the chair scraping loudly against the floorboards. My voice, when it finally emerged, was low and dangerously level, stripped bare of all pleasantries. "How dare you?" I managed, the words tasting like ash.

She traced the lines of the copied text with a trembling finger, the paper feeling like sandpaper under her touch. A fierce, cold energy coiled beneath her skin, vibrating with suppressed fury. I watched the footnotes, the subtle shifts in metaphor, and a sickening realization washed over me. It was *mine*. Every carefully chosen phrase, every intricate plot twist—it was all there, meticulously lifted, stitched together by some other hand. My voice, when it finally emerged, was low and dangerously level, stripped bare of all pleasantries. "How dare you?" I managed, the words tasting like ash.
